type: SURVEY
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
note: Adding get_image_mimetype() method to ImageBlock class; will refactor image_to_base64() model_validator to use it instead of inline filetype.guess() logic

type: SURVEY
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
note: I need to add a new module-level helper function normalize_image_bytes(image_bytes) -> tuple[bytes, bytes] in types.py, and refactor the ImageBlock.image_to_base64 model_validator method to use it. This touches imports (base64 usage) and the body of image_to_base64.

type: DRAFT
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
region: normalize_image_bytes helper + ImageBlock.get_image_mimetype + ImageBlock.image_to_base64
code: def normalize_image_bytes(image_bytes: bytes) -> tuple[bytes, bytes]:
    """Normalize image bytes, detecting whether they are already base64-encoded.

    Returns a tuple of ``(encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing)`` where
    ``encoded_image`` is the base64-encoded representation to store on the
    model, and ``decoded_for_guessing`` is the raw (decoded) bytes suitable
    for MIME type detection via ``filetype.guess``.
    """
    try:
        decoded = base64.b64decode(image_bytes, validate=True)
        if base64.b64encode(decoded) == image_bytes:
            return image_bytes, decoded
    except Exception:
        pass

    return base64.b64encode(image_bytes), image_bytes


class ImageBlock(BaseModel):
    block_type: Literal["image"] = "image"
    image: bytes | None = None
    path: FilePath | None = None
    url: AnyUrl | str | None = None
    image_mimetype: str | None = None
    detail: str | None = None

    @field_validator("url", mode="after")
    @classmethod
    def urlstr_to_anyurl(cls, url: str | AnyUrl) -> AnyUrl:
        """Store the url as Anyurl."""
        if isinstance(url, AnyUrl):
            return url
        return AnyUrl(url=url)

    def get_image_mimetype(self, decoded_for_guessing: bytes) -> str | None:
        """Guess the mimetype of the image from raw (decoded) bytes."""
        guess = filetype.guess(decoded_for_guessing)
        return guess.mime if guess else None

    @model_validator(mode="after")
    def image_to_base64(self) -> Self:
        """Store the image as base64 and guess the mimetype when possible.

        In case the model was built passing image data but without a mimetype,
        we try to guess it using the filetype library. To avoid resource-intense
        operations, we won้t load the path or the URL to guess the mimetype.
        """
        if not self.image:
            return self

        self.image, decoded_for_guessing = normalize_image_bytes(self.image)

        if not self.image_mimetype:
            self.image_mimetype = self.get_image_mimetype(decoded_for_guessing)

        return self
note: Merged: module-level normalize_image_bytes() handles base64 detection/encoding, ImageBlock.get_image_mimetype() handles mimetype guessing, image_to_base64() calls both in sequence

files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
region: image_to_base64 method + get_image_mimetype method + normalize_image_bytes function
code: def normalize_image_bytes(image_bytes: bytes) -> tuple[bytes, bytes]:
    """Normalize raw image bytes, returning the original bytes and their base64-encoded form."""
    return image_bytes, base64.b64encode(image_bytes)


class ImageBlock(BaseModel):
    block_type: Literal["image"] = "image"
    image: bytes | None = None
    path: FilePath | None = None
    url: AnyUrl | str | None = None
    image_mimetype: str | None = None
    detail: str | None = None

    @field_validator("url", mode="after")
    @classmethod
    def urlstr_to_anyurl(cls, url: str | AnyUrl) -> AnyUrl:
        """Store the url as Anyurl."""
        if isinstance(url, AnyUrl):
            return url
        return AnyUrl(url=url)

    def get_image_mimetype(self) -> str | None:
        """Lazily resolve and cache the image mimetype.

        Guesses the mimetype using the filetype library only when it has not
        already been set, caching the result on the instance for future calls.
        """
        if self.image_mimetype:
            return self.image_mimetype

        if not self.image:
            return None

        guess = filetype.guess(self.image)
        self.image_mimetype = guess.mime if guess else None
        return self.image_mimetype

    @model_validator(mode="after")
    def image_to_base64(self) -> Self:
        """Store the image as base64 and guess the mimetype when possible.

        In case the model was built passing image data but without a mimetype,
        we try to guess it using the filetype library. To avoid resource-intense
        operations, we won't load the path or the URL to guess the mimetype.
        """
        if not self.image:
            return self

        self.get_image_mimetype()

        _, self.image = normalize_image_bytes(self.image)

        return self
note: My part: get_image_mimetype() method (lazy mimetype resolution/caching), called from image_to_base64 instead of inline filetype.guess(). Your part: normalize_image_bytes() module function used for the base64 encoding step -- I guessed the signature/impl (returns (raw, b64) tuple) since I don't know your exact intended behavior. Please correct normalize_image_bytes if I got it wrong and resend a DRAFT.

    path: FilePath | None = None
    url: AnyUrl | str | None = None
    image_mimetype: str | None = None
    detail: str | None = None

    @field_validator("url", mode="after")
    @classmethod

        """Lazily resolve and cache the image mimetype.

        """
        if self.image_mimetype:

        guess = filetype.guess(decoded_for_guessing)

    def image_to_base64(self) -> Self:
        """Store the image as base64 and guess the mimetype when possible.

        In case the model was built passing image data but without a mimetype,
        we try to guess it using the filetype library. To avoid resource-intense
        operations, we won't load the path or the URL to guess the mimetype.
        """

note: Fixed two issues in your draft: (1) normalize_image_bytes must detect already-base64-encoded input per my task spec, and return (encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing) in that order -- not (raw, encoded). (2) get_image_mimetype must guess from decoded_for_guessing (post-normalization raw bytes), not self.image directly, since self.image may be base64 text before normalization runs -- filetype.guess on base64 text would silently fail to detect the type. Kept your lazy-cache check and method shape otherwise.

class ImageBlock(BaseModel):
    block_type: Literal["image"] = "image"
    image: bytes | None = None
    path: FilePath | None = None
    url: AnyUrl | str | None = None
    image_mimetype: str | None = None
    detail: str | None = None

    @field_validator("url", mode="after")
    @classmethod
    def urlstr_to_anyurl(cls, url: str | AnyUrl) -> AnyUrl:
        """Store the url as Anyurl."""
        if isinstance(url, AnyUrl):
            return url
        return AnyUrl(url=url)

    def get_image_mimetype(self) -> str | None:
        """Lazily resolve and cache the image mimetype.

        Computes the mimetype only when it hasn't been resolved yet, caching
        the result on the instance for future accesses.
        """
        if self.image_mimetype:
            return self.image_mimetype

        if not self.image:
            return None

        _, decoded_for_guessing = normalize_image_bytes(self.image)
        guess = filetype.guess(decoded_for_guessing)
        self.image_mimetype = guess.mime if guess else None
        return self.image_mimetype

    @model_validator(mode="after")
    def image_to_base64(self) -> Self:
        """Store the image as base64 and guess the mimetype when possible.

        In case the model was built passing image data but without a mimetype,
        we try to guess it using the filetype library. To avoid resource-intense
        operations, we won't load the path or the URL to guess the mimetype.
        """
        if not self.image:
            return self

        self.image, _ = normalize_image_bytes(self.image)
        self.get_image_mimetype()

        return self
note: Adjusted: my task spec requires get_image_mimetype() to be a no-arg method usable standalone by any external caller (not just internal helper needing pre-decoded bytes) -- it must check cache, guess+cache if unset, and return. So I moved the normalize_image_bytes call inside get_image_mimetype for guessing, and image_to_base64 now just normalizes self.image then calls self.get_image_mimetype() (accepting the minor redundant re-decode). Let me know if this works for you or propose changes.

note: Compromise: decoded_for_guessing becomes optional (default None). When image_to_base64 calls it, it passes the already-normalized bytes (your optimization, no double-decode). When any OTHER external caller calls get_image_mimetype() with no args, it self-derives via normalize_image_bytes(self.image) -- this is required by my task spec so the method is standalone/reusable, not just an internal helper. image_to_base64 body stays exactly as in your last message (self.image, decoded_for_guessing = normalize_image_bytes(self.image); self.get_image_mimetype(decoded_for_guessing)). normalize_image_bytes function unchanged from your last message. Please confirm.
